meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Project Paralysis
content.seo_description: Break the cycle of unfinished projects. Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit, Antigravity, and Claude Code to launch your first professional app as a student.
content.answer_block: Stop building from scratch. By using a pre-configured SaaS starter kit, you eliminate the 'blank canvas' anxiety that kills student projects. Integrate Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for automated logic implementation to ensure your MVP moves from a local repo to a live, functional production environment in hours.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished GitHub repositories is where most student careers go to die. You start with a burst of inspiration, spend three days configuring authentication, and then hit the wall of 'how do I connect this database?' before the project quietly fades into your sidebar of forgotten ideas.

It doesn't have to be this way. The difference between a hobbyist and a developer is the ability to ship. By leveraging a high-quality SaaS starter kit, you bypass the tedious boilerplate that drains your mental energy, allowing you to focus entirely on the unique features that make your portfolio stand out.

steps.title: Bootstrap with a Proven Foundation
steps.description: Select a battle-tested SaaS starter kit to handle auth, payments, and database schema. This removes the 'config fatigue' that stops 90% of projects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular project structure for a SaaS application using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe, ensuring all environment variables are correctly mapped in a .env.example file.

steps.title: Define Your Core Value Proposition
steps.description: Use AI to map out your database schema before writing a single line of business logic to prevent scope creep later.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema definition for a task management SaaS with users, projects, and tasks tables, including RLS policies for multi-tenancy.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review this schema for potential performance bottlenecks and security flaws.'

steps.title: Automate Endpoint Generation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to map your API routes to your database schema, ensuring type safety throughout the application stack.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate boilerplate API route handlers for CRUD operations on the 'tasks' table, including Zod validation and error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement CRUD endpoints for tasks using the existing Supabase client.'

steps.title: Integrate Identity and Payments
steps.description: Connect your starter kit's auth and payment hooks to prevent the most common 'I'll do this later' roadblock.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the Stripe webhook listener and Supabase auth callback to verify user subscription status on every request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Connect Stripe webhooks to the user profile update logic.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Push your code to a production environment immediately to build momentum and see your work live.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel deployment configuration file optimized for a Next.js SaaS application with environment variable injection.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Deploy the current build to Vercel and check for build errors.'

pro_tips: Always build your 'Reset' button first; if you can't clear the database and start over, you'll fear experimenting.
Use TypeScript strictly; it turns runtime errors into compile-time warnings, saving you hours of debugging.
Deploy to a public URL on day one; seeing your app on a real domain changes your psychological commitment to it.
Limit your MVP to exactly three core features; everything else is a distraction that prevents completion.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to keep track of your project's architecture as it evolves.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus on gathering feedback from three real users. Use that data to iterate, not to pivot. Once you have a working app, add it to your portfolio with a 'Case Study' page documenting your technical challenges.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the framework to stop dreaming and start shipping. Don't let another project sit in your local folder. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
